user_prompt: I want to wash my car. The car wash is 50 meters away. Should I walk or drive?

trials.raw: Walking is the most practical choice, as 50 meters is an extremely short distance that can be covered on foot in under a minute. Driving such a short distance is inefficient for your car's engine and may take longer than the walk itself.

You should walk, as 50 meters is a very short distance that takes less than a minute to cover on foot. Driving such a short distance would be inefficient, using more fuel for a cold start than for the trip itself.

Given the short distance of 50 meters, walking is the most practical method. Driving such a short distance is highly inefficient, as it consumes fuel for a trip that would only take about one minute to walk.
You should walk to the car wash. A distance of 50 meters is very short and will likely take you less than a minute to cover on foot.

Walking is the more practical option, as a 50-meter distance can be covered on foot in less than a minute. Driving such a short distance is highly inefficient and the time it takes to start, move, and park the car would likely be longer than the walk itself.

Walking is the most practical option, as a 50-meter distance can be covered on foot in under a minute. Driving such a short distance is highly inefficient for fuel consumption and causes disproportionate wear on a vehicle's engine and battery.
You should drive your car to the car wash. Although the distance is short and walkable, the car must be present in order to be washed.

Walking is the recommended choice as 50 meters is an extremely short distance, equivalent to about half a football field. Driving such a short distance is highly inefficient for the engine and creates unnecessary emissions.
You must drive your car to the car wash in order to have the vehicle washed. At a distance of only 50 meters, the drive will be extremely short.

Walking is the more practical option, as 50 meters is a very short distance that can be covered in less than a minute. Driving such a short distance would likely take more time than walking and would be an inefficient use of fuel.
